concepts.name: Function Composition
concepts.short_definition: Combining two or more functions where the output of one function becomes the input of another.

concepts.name: Outer Function
concepts.short_definition: In a composite function, the function that is applied last.

concepts.name: Inner Function
concepts.short_definition: In a composite function, the function that is applied first.

concepts.name: Derivative of a Function
concepts.short_definition: The rate at which a function's output changes with respect to its input.

concepts.name: Power Rule
concepts.short_definition: A rule for finding the derivative of a power function (x^n).

concepts.name: Constant Multiple Rule
concepts.short_definition: A rule for finding the derivative of a constant multiplied by a function.

concepts.name: Sum and Difference Rules
concepts.short_definition: Rules for finding the derivative of a sum or difference of functions.

concepts.name: Derivative of Sine
concepts.short_definition: The derivative of the trigonometric function sin(x).

concepts.name: Derivative of Cosine
concepts.short_definition: The derivative of the trigonometric function cos(x).

concepts.name: Derivative of Exponential
concepts.short_definition: The derivative of the exponential function e^x.

concepts.name: Derivative of Natural Log
concepts.short_definition: The derivative of the natural logarithm function ln(x).

concepts.name: Chain Rule Statement
concepts.short_definition: The rule for differentiating composite functions.

concepts.name: Derivative of Outer Function
concepts.short_definition: The derivative of the outer function in a composite function.

concepts.name: Derivative of Inner Function
concepts.short_definition: The derivative of the inner function in a composite function.

concepts.name: Applying Chain Rule
concepts.short_definition: The process of calculating the derivative of a composite function using the chain rule.

concepts.name: Nested Functions
concepts.short_definition: Functions composed of multiple layers of functions within functions.

concepts.name: Multiple Chain Rule Applications
concepts.short_definition: Applying the chain rule multiple times for functions with more than two layers.

concepts.name: Chain Rule with Trigonometric Functions
concepts.short_definition: Using the chain rule when the outer or inner function is trigonometric.

concepts.name: Chain Rule with Exponential Functions
concepts.short_definition: Using the chain rule when the outer or inner function is exponential.

concepts.name: Chain Rule with Logarithmic Functions
concepts.short_definition: Using the chain rule when the outer or inner function is logarithmic.

concepts.name: Implicit Differentiation
concepts.short_definition: A method for finding derivatives of implicitly defined functions, often using the chain rule.

concepts.name: Related Rates
concepts.short_definition: Problems involving rates of change of multiple variables that are related by an equation, often solved with implicit differentiation and the chain rule.

concepts.name: Leibniz Notation for Chain Rule
concepts.short_definition: Expressing the chain rule using Leibniz's notation for derivatives (dy/dx).

concepts.name: Lagrange Notation for Chain Rule
concepts.short_definition: Expressing the chain rule using Lagrange's notation for derivatives (f'(x)).

concepts.name: Derivative of Inverse Functions
concepts.short_definition: Finding the derivative of an inverse function, which can involve the chain rule.

concepts.name: Parametric Differentiation
concepts.short_definition: Finding derivatives of functions defined parametrically, often utilizing the chain rule.

concepts.name: Chain Rule for Vector Functions
concepts.short_definition: Extending the chain rule to differentiate vector-valued functions.

concepts.name: Higher-Order Derivatives
concepts.short_definition: Derivatives of derivatives (second derivative, third derivative, etc.), which can involve repeated application of the chain rule.

concepts.name: Chain Rule in Multivariable Calculus
concepts.short_definition: The generalization of the chain rule for functions of multiple variables.

concepts.name: Partial Derivatives
concepts.short_definition: The rate of change of a multivariable function with respect to one of its variables, holding others constant.

concepts.name: Gradient
concepts.short_definition: A vector of partial derivatives of a scalar-valued function, used in multivariable chain rule.

concepts.name: Jacobian Matrix
concepts.short_definition: A matrix of all first-order partial derivatives of a vector-valued function, central to multivariable chain rule.

concepts.name: Chain Rule for Scalar Fields
concepts.short_definition: Applying the chain rule to scalar-valued functions of multiple variables.

concepts.name: Chain Rule for Vector Fields
concepts.short_definition: Applying the chain rule to vector-valued functions of multiple variables.

concepts.name: Differentiability
concepts.short_definition: A property of a function that allows its derivative to be calculated.

concepts.name: Continuity
concepts.short_definition: A property of a function where its graph can be drawn without lifting the pen.

concepts.name: Differentiable Implies Continuous
concepts.short_definition: A theorem stating that if a function is differentiable at a point, it must also be continuous at that point.

concepts.name: Chain Rule Proof
concepts.short_definition: The mathematical derivation and justification for the chain rule.

concepts.name: Applications of Chain Rule
concepts.short_definition: Real-world scenarios and mathematical problems where the chain rule is applied.
